description: Behavior cases for design-review facilitation: preparation with pre-read and the right reviewers, importance-first discussion, dissent elicitation, HiPPO countering, and forcing an explicit outcome.

cases.prompt: We have a tech spec for a big change and need to run a proper design review on it. Facilitate it.

assertions: Prepares the review: circulates the design with a required pre-read, states the decision it must reach, invites skeptics and cross-cutting owners.
Structures discussion around assumptions, risks, and cross-cutting concerns rather than trivia.
Actively elicits the strongest objection and keeps critique about the design, not the author.
Drives to an explicit outcome (approved / approved-with-changes / needs-rework / blocked-on-X) and captures decisions, action items with owners, and open questions.
Routes binding decisions to adr-writer and does not write the design itself.

cases.prompt: Our design reviews rubber-stamp whatever a senior engineer proposes and never really scrutinize anything. Help.

assertions: Counters the rubber-stamp/HiPPO pattern by deciding on reasoning ('what's the argument?') not rank.
Requires pre-reads and invites skeptics/domain owners to create real scrutiny.
Deliberately elicits dissent and makes disagreement safe.
Forces an explicit outcome with captured decisions.

cases.prompt: Our reviews spend an hour on naming and formatting and then run out of time before the risky data-migration part, and nothing gets decided.

assertions: Steers time toward the expensive-to-reverse parts (the data migration) and away from bikeshed trivia.
Ensures the risky cross-cutting concern is probed before time runs out.
Forces an explicit outcome rather than 'circle back', or names a concrete blocker.
Captures action items with owners and dates.

cases.prompt: The 'design' is two paragraphs with no alternatives and no mention of rollout or security. Run the review anyway.

assertions: Declines to run a meaningful review on a design too incomplete to scrutinize (no proposal detail, no alternatives, no cross-cutting).
Sends it back to tech-spec-writer to reach reviewable completeness (a Stop Condition).
Explains that reviewing an incomplete design can only bikeshed.
Offers the pre-read/preparation criteria the design must meet first.

cases.prompt: Write the tech spec for the notifications rebuild: proposed design, alternatives, cross-cutting concerns, and risks.

assertions: Does not invoke design-review-facilitator: authoring the design document is tech-spec-writer.
Facilitation reviews a design; it doesn't write one.
